# Transcrição: How_to_sound_more_polite

- **Fonte:** How_to_sound_more_polite
- **Duração:** 01:10
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:10]

If I talk like this and I all of a sudden have this kind of melody in my voice and I speak to you like this, do I sound polite? I don't do I. All of a sudden I don't sound polite. And what we then start to realise in that simple little example is the melody that we use in our voice indicates a lot of things. Right now the melody that lives underneath my voice hopefully indicates that I'm a friendly person. It's melody in our voice that makes us sound more friendly and polite. So if you want to sound polite add a little more melody to your voice. And when you speak to people instead of just going, hi it's good to see you. It's hey it's good to see you. And one of the easiest ways to add more melody to our voice is to smile. When you smile more as you smile naturally adds more melody to your voice. There's visual indicators for polite and there's auditory indicators for polite. And the visual indicator for polite is a smile. When you smile people immediately assume, oh this person's friendly, they're polite, they're nice. And naturally what happens when you smile more is there's more melody. So all of a sudden you're indicating to people visually and auditory wise that hey I'm friendly and I'm polite.
